# DACS — Project Guidelines

## What This Project Is

Research prototype and academic paper for **Dynamic Attentional Context Scoping (DACS)** — a mechanism where a multi-agent orchestrator enters agent-triggered focus sessions to isolate per-agent context during steering interactions. Target: arxiv preprint + workshop paper (NeurIPS/ICLR/AAMAS), 8-week timeline starting April 2026.

Full plan: [ACTION_PLAN.md](../ACTION_PLAN.md)

## Stack

- **Language:** Python (3.11+)
- **LLM calls:** Direct API (OpenAI or Anthropic) — no agent framework wrappers
- **No external orchestration frameworks** (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.) — the harness is intentionally minimal (~300 lines) to keep full observability over context window contents
- **Paper:** LaTeX (arxiv template) in `paper/`

## Repository Structure

```
src/          Core mechanism (registry, protocols, context_builder, orchestrator)
agents/       Stub agent implementations (base, code_writer, research, data_processor)
experiments/  Experiment harness, task suite, metrics
results/      Auto-generated CSVs and plots — do not edit manually
notes/        Literature review notes and formal definition (Markdown)
docs/         Architecture diagram and interface specs (Markdown)
paper/        LaTeX paper (main.tex + figures/)
```

## Key Domain Terminology

Always use these names consistently:

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `Orchestrator` | The single LLM managing all agents and user interaction |
| `Registry` / `R` | Lightweight per-agent state snapshots (≤200 tokens each) |
| `Focus context` / `F(aᵢ)` | Full context of agent `aᵢ` injected during a focus session |
| `SteeringRequest` | Structured message an agent emits when it needs orchestrator input |
| `REGISTRY mode` | Orchestrator holds registry summaries only |
| `FOCUS(aᵢ) mode` | Orchestrator holds `F(aᵢ)` + compressed registry, steers `aᵢ` |
| `context pollution` | The problem DACS solves — all agent threads competing in one context window |

## Core Constraints

- **Observability is paramount.** Every LLM call must log the exact token contents of the context window. This is the central experiment variable. Never abstract away what goes into a prompt.
- **Token budget must be enforced deterministically.** `context_builder.py` must count tokens before every call and hard-cap at `T`. Do not rely on the LLM provider to truncate.
- **Baseline must be a fair comparison.** The flat-context baseline orchestrator uses the identical code path as DACS except focus mode is disabled. No other differences.
- **Experiment results are ground truth.** Do not modify files under `results/` — they are generated by `experiments/run_experiment.py`.

## Experiment Design (reference)

- **Conditions:** DACS vs flat-context baseline
- **Agent counts tested:** N = 3, 5, 10
- **Trials:** 10 per condition per N → 60 runs total
- **Key metrics:** steering accuracy (0/1), wrong-agent contamination, context size at steering time, user responsiveness
- **Task suite:** 5 scenarios × 3 decision points each, with known-correct answers defined in `experiments/task_suite.py`

## Paper Framing

- DACS is **not** a context compression algorithm (that's AFM/AOI). It is an **active, asymmetric, agent-triggered context isolation mechanism**.
- The closest prior work is AFM (arxiv 2511.12712) and AOI (arxiv 2512.13956) — always be precise about the distinction.
- OpenClaw is used as motivation/context in the paper introduction, not as implementation platform.
